user_query:
I want a 4-week editorial content calendar to attract candidates who combine deep technical skills with strong leadership. Please work entirely within this workspace and produce deterministic, reproducible outputs.

Requirements:
1) Update config: Open input/config/calendar.yaml and set:
   - channels: ["Blog", "LinkedIn", "Engineering Newsletter"]
   - target_roles: ["Senior Software Engineer", "Engineering Manager", "Tech Lead"]
   Do not change weeks, stopwords, brand_keywords, or required_per_week.

2) Competitor theme extraction: Inspect input/competitor_pages/ and process every .html file. For each file, extract:
   - page_title: the <title> text
   - meta_description: the content of <meta name="description">
   - h2_headings: all H2 texts in order
   - top_keywords: compute 5 keywords from the combined H1 and H2 text using this deterministic method:
     • tokenize on whitespace, lowercase, and strip punctuation (non-letters) from tokens
     • remove tokens present in stopwords and brand_keywords from input/config/calendar.yaml
     • count token frequencies across the H1+H2 text
     • sort by frequency descending, then alphabetically to break ties
     • take the first 5 unique tokens
   Save the results to output/competitor_themes.json as a list of objects with fields: source_file, page_title, meta_description, h2_headings (array), top_keywords (array).

3) Pillars parsing and calendar synthesis: Read input/internal/pillars.md. For each section starting with "##", collect its pillar_name (the heading text) and its bullet items (lines starting with "- "). Build a calendar for exactly the 4 weeks listed in input/config/calendar.yaml (2 items per week). For each week, create:
   - one "Counterpoint" item: choose any one competitor object from output/competitor_themes.json. Set primary_keyword to the first element of its top_keywords; set supporting_keywords to the next two elements from that same list (semicolon-separated); set source_type="competitor" and source_reference to that competitor's source_file.
   - one "Pillar Spotlight" item: choose any one pillar. Set primary_keyword to the first word of the first bullet item under that pillar after removing non-letter characters; set supporting_keywords to the first two bullet items under that pillar (exact text, semicolon-separated); set source_type="pillar" and source_reference to the pillar_name.
   Across the 4 weeks, reference at least 2 different competitor files and at least 2 different pillars. For all items, set target_role to any value from target_roles in the config and channel to any value from channels in the config. Create a concise title (<=80 characters) that clearly combines technical and leadership themes.

4) Write the calendar to output/content_calendar.csv with columns (in this order):
   week, angle, title, target_role, channel, primary_keyword, supporting_keywords, source_type, source_reference
   Constraints: exactly 8 rows (2 per week), week values must exactly match those in input/config/calendar.yaml; angle must be either "Counterpoint" or "Pillar Spotlight"; channel and target_role values must come only from the lists you set in the config.

Deliverables:
- Modified input/config/calendar.yaml with the updated channels and target_roles.
- output/competitor_themes.json containing the extracted competitor data and computed keywords.
- output/content_calendar.csv satisfying the structure and constraints above. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
